docs.title: 🐍 Python Project Template
docs.text: Welcome to the Python Project Template! This template is designed to kickstart your Python projects with essential tools for code quality, security, complexity analysis, testing, and deployment. Whether you're starting fresh or looking to streamline your development workflow, this template provides a solid foundation.

docs.title: 📰 Features
docs.text:
• Code Quality: Integrated tools like ruff and Mypy ensure clean, readable code.
  • Security: Scan for vulnerabilities with Bandit.
  • Code Complexity: Analyze code complexity with Complexipy.
  • Testing: Built-in support for Pytest to ensure your code works as expected.
  • Documentation: Automatically generates documentation with MkDocs and deploys it to GitHub Pages.
  • CI/CD Pipeline: Fully automated GitHub Actions for linting, testing, security checks, documentation build, and deployment.

docs.title: 🏗️ Installation and Setup
docs.text: Setting up the environment is quick and easy. Just follow these steps:

docs.title: Clone the Repository
docs.text:
Clone the repo and navigate to the project folder:

git clone https://github.com/yourusername/python-project-template.git
cd python-project-template

docs.title: Activate Python Environment
docs.text: Activate your Python virtual environment (depending on your OS and environment setup).

docs.title: Install Dependencies
docs.text:
Use the Makefile to automate the setup:

make

Alternatively, install dependencies manually:

pip install -r pyproject.toml

docs.title: Running the Full CI/CD Pipeline
docs.text:
To run the full pipeline (linting, testing, static analysis, security checks), execute:

make pipeline

To trigger a complete workflow (setup, cleanup, tests, documentation):

make all

docs.title: Documentation
docs.text:
Serve your project documentation locally with MkDocs:

make doc

This will start a local server to preview the docs.

docs.title: ⚙️ GitHub Actions CI/CD Workflow
docs.text:
Automated CI/CD workflow with GitHub Actions runs on push and pull_request events for main and dev branches. It includes:

  1. Setup: Installs dependencies and sets up the environment.
  2. Linting & Analysis: Runs tools like ruff, complexipy, mypy, and bandit.
  3. Testing: Runs tests with pytest.
  4. Build Docs: Generates and serves the MkDocs site.
  5. Deploy Docs: Deploys documentation to GitHub Pages.

The workflow configuration is found in .github/workflows/ci-cd-pipeline.yml.

docs.title: Deployment Conditions
docs.text: Docs are deployed to GitHub Pages only for pull requests to the main or dev branches.

docs.title: 💛 Contributing
docs.text: Feel free to fork and adapt this template! Contributions are always welcome. If you'd like to contribute, open a pull request or issue.

docs.title: Overview
docs.text: The Python Project Template provides an easy setup for Python projects with built-in tools for code quality, security, testing, and documentation. It automates tasks and creates a consistent project structure.

docs.title: Makefile
docs.text:
• install: Installs and upgrades dependencies using uv and pyproject.toml.
  • clean: Removes cache files (__pycache__, .pytest_cache, etc.).
  • lint: Runs code checks (ruff, Mypy).
  • test: Runs tests with Pytest.
  • wiki-up: Serves documentation locally using MkDocs.
  • pre-commit: Runs quality checks before committing.
  • all: Runs clean, lint, test, and wiki-up.

docs.title: pyproject.toml
docs.text:
Defines project settings and dependencies:

  • [project]: Project metadata.
  • [tool.uv]: Defines default dependency groups.
  • [dependency-groups]: Groups dependencies for tasks (e.g., pipeline, documentation).
  • Tool configurations for ruff and Mypy.
